mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135195.0 as of April 2013 shows ongoing recovery trajectory
failure_condition: Payroll growth acceleration does not precede positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remaining below 0.25% signals an extended zero-lower-bound monetary regime that suppresses risk-free rates and forces capital into equities as yield-seeking behavior, producing sustained positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of March 2013 confirms ZLB regime
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals strengthening consumer and construction demand, which increases corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 917.0 and RSAFS at 418284.0 as of February-March 2013 show demand recovery
failure_condition: Joint HOUST and RSAFS acceleration does not precede positive 20-day returns in at least 58% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below 99.0 signals weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side constraints, which compress profit margins and reduce equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.0446 as of February 2013 near recent lows
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month declines > 0.5% do not precede negative 5-day returns in at least 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 7.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which dampens aggregate demand and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% as of March 2013 remains elevated post-crisis
failure_condition: UNRATE increases > 0.3pp do not precede negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of instances, or mean forward return in rising UNRATE periods is not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% annually signals monetary tightening pressure that forces the Fed to raise rates, increasing discount rates on equity cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 232.77 as of February 2013 implies ~1.5% YoY inflation
regime_hint: inflation acceleration or tightening cycle initiation
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5% does not precede negative 60-day returns in at least 54% of instances, or correlation with forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust economic expansion that increases corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13665.4 and RSAFS at 418284.0 show recovery momentum
failure_condition: Joint GDPC1 acceleration and RSAFS growth do not precede positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) rising above 99.5 combined with low unemployment (UNRATE < 7.5%) signals tight supply-demand balance and rising wage pressures that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.0446 and UNRATE at 7.6% as of early 2013
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO > 99.5 and UNRATE < 7.5% do not precede negative 20-day returns in at least 53% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression.
